title: Comastoma traillianum
taxonomy: Comastoma traillianum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant is native to Western China and is a type of alpine herbaceous perennial. It is often found growing in mountainous regions with rocky or stony soil. The classification of Comastoma traillianum includes being a member of the genus Comastoma and the order Gentianales. The plant's specific classification is further defined by its unique characteristics and growth habits.
uses: Comastoma traillianum is known to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal remedies to treat different health conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are used in folk medicine to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The leaves are also believed to have antibacterial properties, which may help in treating infections. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.

intro: Comastoma traillianum, also known as Traill's comastoma, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Gentianaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Comastoma traillianum include gentiopicrin, gentiogenin, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Comastoma traillianum is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
